name: Agendor API
description:
# Introduction

Welcome to our API docs! Agendor is a CRM system made to streamline the
sales process of teams of inside and outside sales. With our API, you
can integrate your existing systems with Agendor and make your sales
team even more efficient. Check out some help articles our team has
written about integrating with our API (in Portuguese):

* [Do you have examples of how to use the Agendor API?](https://ajuda.agendor.com.br/integracoes/voces-tem-exemplos-de-como-utilizar-a-api-do-agendor)
* [Can I integrate Agendor to my current system?](https://ajuda.agendor.com.br/integracoes/o-agendor-pode-ser-integrado-ao-meu-sistema-atual)

## Structure

Our API is a RESTful JSON API, which means:

* The endpoints are structured around *resources*, identified by URIs;
* HTTP *verbs* are used to act differently on the same resource
and HTTP *status codes* are used to signal the result of an operation;
* We expect body parameters in JSON and also respond in JSON.

The body of succesful responses will have up to three main root level
properties:

* `data`: actual data of the resource you're working with, will always
be present;
* `meta`: metadata about the resource, for instance total size of a
collection of results;
* `links`: links to related resources, which can be used to navigate
through the API.

The failure responses will be signaled by HTTP status codes and also
have error details under the `errors` property in the response body.

## Authentication

<!-- ReDoc-Inject: <security-definitions> -->

To authenticate your requests, you need an API token. You can find
your token by logging into Agendor, and going to
[Menu > Integrações](https://web.agendor.com.br/sistema/integracoes).
This token is like your password, so keep it secret!

Add this token to the `Authorization` header of your requests, using
the `Token` scheme, for example, in cURL:

```bash
curl -H 'Authorization: Token your-token-here' https://api.agendor.com.br/v3/users/me
```

In PHP, you might do:

```php
$curl = curl_init();

curl_setopt($curl, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, array("Authorization: Token your-token-here"));
curl_setopt($curl, CURLOPT_URL, "https://api.agendor.com.br/v3/users/me");

$response = curl_exec($curl);
```

In case of failed authentication, the API will respond with a `401`
status code and the following body:

```json
{
  "errors": [
    "Token could not be authenticated"
  ]
}
```

If you're getting this error, you might be using an invalid token or
your headers are not set up properly. Check if your Agendor account and
user are active and if you have included your token in the `Authorization`
header, preceded by the word `Token`.

## Pagination

In the endpoints where you might `GET` a collection of resources, they
will be paginated. By default, `10` items are returned per page, but
you can increase the page size up to `100`.

To navigate through the results, use the `page` parameter, starting
from page `1`. The total count of results will be included in the
`meta` property of the response and links for the following and
preceding pages will be  included under `links`:

```bash
GET https://api.agendor.com.br/v3/organizations?page=2

{
  "data": [...
  ],
  "meta": {
    "totalCount": 100
  },
  "links": {
    "self": "https://api.agendor.com.br/v3/organizations?page=2",
    "prev": "https://api.agendor.com.br/v3/organizations?page=1",
    "next": "https://api.agendor.com.br/v3/organizations?page=3"
  }
}
```
